name: switcher-container
role: Container that lays its children side-by-side (row) above a width threshold and stacks them (column) below. Universal for two-up content blocks that need to remain readable as the container narrows — split heroes, two-column comparisons, side-by-side cards.

threshold.note: Container-width threshold below which the row stacks. narrow=30em (~480px), standard=40em (~640px), wide=60em (~960px).

gap.note: Gap between children in both row and column mode.

align.note: Cross-axis alignment in row mode (align-items). Column mode uses stretch by default.

meta.containerQueryContract: switcher-container declares container-type: inline-size. v3.G.11 container-query convention applies.
meta.browserSupportBaseline: Container queries are Baseline 2024. Older browsers degrade to the row layout always — the operator can choose to ship a stacked fallback via @supports gating instead, if a always-stacked fallback is preferred on legacy browsers.
meta.differenceFromSidebar: prim-sidebar has TWO distinct slots (sidebar + main) with different roles. prim-switcher has N equal children that all flip together. Use prim-sidebar when one child is structurally a sidebar; use prim-switcher when the children are peers (e.g., two equal columns).
meta.cssRecipe: container-type: inline-size; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: var(...); @container (inline-size >= threshold) { flex-direction: row; & > * { flex: 1; } }
meta.flexChildrenInRowMode: In row mode, children get flex: 1 to share inline-size equally. Operators who want unequal splits set explicit flex-basis on the child elements after composing the primitive.
